join_order.notes: Build compact hash map adsh_code→name_code from sub filtered by fy=2022. Cap=65536 (next_power_of_2(27K*2)). Slot={int32_t adsh_code, int32_t name_code}=8B. Total ~512KB, fits L2. Use sub_fy_zone_map to skip sub blocks where fy=2022 cannot exist. Probe during num scan for each qualifying num row.

join_order.probe: collected_buffer(qualifying num rows matched to sub)
join_order.notes: Self-join of collected buffer against max_map to filter rows where value==max_value. Key=packed uint64_t((uint32_t)adsh_code<<32|(uint32_t)tag_code). Cap=262144 (next_power_of_2(94K*2)). Slot={uint64_t key, double max_val}=16B. Total ~4MB, fits L3.

filters.notes: Load uom_dict, find pure_code at runtime (C2: never hardcode). Zone-map-guided scan: num sorted by uom → use num_uom_zone_map to skip blocks where block_max < pure_code or block_min > pure_code. Skips ~99.7% of 395 blocks (~1-2 blocks qualifying, ~100K rows to scan).
NULL sentinel is NaN. Apply together with uom filter in fused loop. No separate pass needed.
Use sub_fy_zone_map (9 blocks of 10K rows) to skip sub blocks where fy=2022 cannot exist. ~27K qualifying rows.

data_structures.sub_fy_map: compact_hash_map(cap=65536, slot={int32_t adsh_code, int32_t name_code}, sentinel=INT32_MIN, bounded_probing per C24). Pre-sized for 27K filtered rows per P23.
data_structures.qualifying_buffer: flat_array of struct{int32_t adsh_code; int32_t tag_code; double value; int32_t name_code} collecting all num rows that pass uom+value filters and probe sub_fy_map. Expected ~94K entries, reserve 150K. Zero-copy from mmap columns.
data_structures.max_map: compact_hash_map(cap=262144, key=uint64_t packed(adsh_code,tag_code), value=double, sentinel key=UINT64_MAX, init to -DBL_MAX). Sized for ~94K groups per P23. ~4MB total, fits L3.
data_structures.result_buffer: flat_array of struct{double value; string name; string tag} for final decoded results before top-100 sort

execution_steps.name: build_sub_fy_map
execution_steps.description: Scan sub table (86K rows). Load sub/fy.bin, sub/adsh.bin, sub/name.bin via mmap. Use sub_fy_zone_map to skip blocks. Filter fy==2022. Insert {adsh_code → name_code} into sub_fy_map hash table. Single-threaded (86K rows, negligible cost).

execution_steps.name: zone_map_scan_num_collect
execution_steps.description: Load num_uom_zone_map. Find contiguous block range where pure_code in [block_min, block_max]. For each qualifying block: mmap num/uom.bin + num/value.bin + num/adsh.bin + num/tag.bin. Late materialization: filter uom==pure_code AND !isnan(value) first (only 2 columns), then load adsh+tag for passing rows. Probe sub_fy_map for adsh_code → get name_code. If match, append {adsh_code, tag_code, value, name_code} to qualifying_buffer. Morsel-driven parallel scan over qualifying blocks.

execution_steps.name: build_max_map
execution_steps.description: Single pass over qualifying_buffer. For each row, compute key=packed(adsh_code,tag_code). Probe max_map; if key not found insert with value; if found update if value > current max. Single-threaded (94K rows, trivially fast).

execution_steps.name: filter_max_join
execution_steps.description: Second pass over qualifying_buffer. For each row, probe max_map with packed key. Keep row if value == max_map[key] (exact double equality). Collect surviving rows.

execution_steps.name: topk_sort
execution_steps.description: Decode adsh→name via name_dict[name_code], tag via tag_dict[tag_code]. Build result tuples (value, name, tag). Use std::partial_sort or min-heap for top-100 (P6). Sort key: (value DESC, name ASC, tag ASC).

parallelism.notes: Zone-map skip reduces num scan to ~1-2 blocks (~100K rows). Parallel scan still applied for robustness. Sub build and aggregation phases are single-threaded (tiny cardinalities). Thread-local qualifying_buffers merged after scan.

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — find contiguous block range where uom=pure_code. Layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=395][ZoneBlock<int16_t>×395]. Block i starts at row i*100000. Skip if block_max < pure_code or block_min > pure_code.
skip_blocks — skip sub blocks where fy=2022 cannot exist. 9 blocks of 10K rows.

aggregation_notes: GROUP BY (adsh_code, tag_code) MAX(value). Key packed as uint64_t for single-comparison equality check. No division/precision issues for MAX(double) per C29 (no SUM/AVG). Initialize max values to -DBL_MAX.

output.sort_strategy: partial_sort or min-heap (P6), O(n log k) where k=100

correctness_notes: C2: Load uom_dict at runtime, find pure_code via find_code(). Never hardcode.
C9: Hash table caps use next_power_of_2(N*2). sub_fy_map cap=65536, max_map cap=262144.
C15: max_map key includes BOTH adsh_code AND tag_code (packed uint64). GROUP BY both columns.
C18: Decode name_code via name_dict[name_code], tag_code via tag_dict[tag_code] at output time only.
C19: Zone-map skip only on num.uom (sorted by uom) and sub.fy (zone-map indexed). Never on unsorted columns.
C20: Use std::fill or explicit loop to initialize hash table sentinels. NEVER memset for multi-byte sentinels.
C24: All hash table probes use bounded for-loop: for(uint64_t p=0; p<cap; ++p). No unbounded while.
C29: Q2 uses MAX(value) not SUM/AVG — double comparison is safe. No int64_t cents needed.
P6: LIMIT 100 — use partial_sort or min-heap, not full sort.
P11: sub_adsh_hash pre-built index not used here — sub only 86K rows, build cost negligible vs mmap overhead.
P23: max_map sized on filtered buffer cardinality (~94K), not raw num table size (39.4M).
